moves.headline: Conditions on the individual twins, not tract averages
moves.detail: Each twin gets condition flags (obesity BMI≥30 measured, diabetes diagnosed, hypertension measured) via BRFSS 2024 + NHANES 2021–23 donor matching on age × sex × race × income with a home-state geographic ring — so prevalence reflects the workforce's own demographics, fixing the ecological weakness.

moves.headline: Burden = affected covered adults × MEPS incremental cost
moves.detail: National prevalence × covered adults × the per-condition MEPS annual incremental cost range.

moves.headline: Real comorbidity
moves.detail: Per-twin overlap across obesity/diabetes/hypertension — impossible with ecological prevalence. Conditions may be summed within a person, never across the three.

limitations: Modeled imputation (donor hot-deck + conditional fusion), not claims — tier=modeled.
Employed-workforce burden, NOT general population (no PLACES calibration, on purpose).
Per-state prevalence only where ≥50 twins; smaller states omitted.
Worker condition rates applied to covered adults (including adult dependents).
